mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz C220 / C230 petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned C220/C230 petrol engine typically costs £1,500–£6,500 supply only depending on the engine generation. M111 four-cylinder engines sit at the lower end (£1,500–£4,500), while M112 and M272 V6 units run £2,000–£6,000. Replacing the engine in a Mercedes C-Class could cost £3,500 or more. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 depending on workshop location. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz C220 / C230 petrol engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine generation your C-Class has. The M111 (W202 C220) is generally robust but suffers from timing chain stretch after 160,000–200,000 km and oil leaks from the valve cover gasket. The M112 V6 (W202 C230) is smooth but prone to crankshaft pulley failure and oil leaks. The M271 (W203/W204) has timing chain and camshaft adjuster issues. The M272 V6 (W203) is known for balance shaft sprocket wear. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz C220 / C230 petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch on the M111 (W202 C220), crankshaft pulley failure on the M112 V6 (W202 C230), balance shaft sprocket wear on the M272 V6 (W203 C230), and timing chain issues on the M271 (W203/W204). Oil leaks from valve cover gaskets and camshaft adjuster solenoids are common across all engines.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz C220 / C230 petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold: if your C-Class is worth £5,000–£10,000 and a recon engine is £3,500–£4,500 (35–90% of the car's value), assess the overall condition carefully. Well-maintained examples with good bodywork and interior justify replacement spend. Tired examples with significant rust may not. W202 and W203 models are becoming classics — a good engine rebuild can add value if the rest of the car is in good condition.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz C220 / C230 petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: W202 C220 (1994–2000) is M111.961 (2.2L I4, 150 PS), W202 C230 (1997–2000) is M112 E28 (2.8L V6, 197–204 PS), W203 C230 Kompressor (2000–2004) is M271 (1.8L I4 Kompressor, 192 PS), W203 C230 V6 (2004–2007) is M272 E25 (2.5L V6, 204 PS), W204 C230 (2007–2015) is M271 (1.8L I4 turbo, 184 PS). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain, or does the engine need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A timing chain replacement (£800–£2,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic failure requiring a full engine replacement. Address rattling noises immediately to prevent severe engine damage. At the first sign of rattling on startup, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil changes and proper maintenance significantly extend chain life.

eraMap.title: The C220 / C230 PETROL THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Durable 4-cylinder — timing chain stretch and oil leaks from ~160,000 km
197–204 bhp V6 — smooth but prone to crank pulley failure and oil leaks
192 bhp supercharged four — coarse and noisy; timing chain and cam adjuster issues
204 bhp V6 — balance shaft sprocket failure is a known weakness
184 bhp turbo four — improved over Kompressor but timing chain issues remain

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz C220 / C230 Petrol Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market rates for C-Class petrol engines. Replacing the engine in a Mercedes C-Class could cost £3,500 or more, especially with a brand-new engine and dealership labour. Used and reconditioned engines offer significant savings. M111 and M271 four-cylinder engines generally cost less than M112 and M272 V6 units.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a C-Class petrol engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). V6 engines (M112/M272) require more time than four-cylinder units due to the tighter engine bay.

cta.label: → Compare C220 / C230 petrol engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch (M111 — W202 1994–2000)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking, poor performance, engine warning light. Natural chain elongation over time and mileage, typically after 160,000–200,000 km. Worn timing chain guides and tensioners, oil starvation due to poor maintenance, and high-mileage wear on the hydraulic tensioner mechanism are all contributing factors. A stretched timing chain can jump teeth, causing severe engine damage including bent valves and piston damage.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on startup, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The M111's timing chain is a known weak point at higher mileages. Address rattling noises immediately to prevent severe engine damage. Regular oil changes and proper maintenance significantly extend chain life. At the first sign of rattling on startup, book an inspection immediately — preventative chain replacement is far cheaper than a full engine rebuild.

problems.title: Crankshaft Pulley Failure (M112 V6 — W202 C230 1997–2000)
problems.symptoms: Visible rubber separation on the crankshaft pulley, vibrations, noise from the front of the engine, loss of power steering or charging. The signature failure of this series of engines is the destruction of the crankshaft pulley.
problems.typicalMileage: 80,000–120,000 miles — the rubber bonding in the pulley disintegrates with age.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, the pulley can separate completely, damaging belts and other components.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The M112 V6's crankshaft pulley is a known failure point. Regular visual inspection of the pulley for rubber separation is recommended. If you notice vibrations or noise from the front of the engine, have the pulley inspected immediately. Replacing the pulley is far cheaper than repairing damage caused by a complete failure.

problems.title: Balance Shaft Sprocket Wear (M272 V6 — W203 C230 V6 2004–2007)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light, rough running, poor performance, timing chain noise. The balance shaft sprocket wears prematurely, causing timing chain issues. This is a well-documented failure on early M272 engines.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — the issue can appear at relatively low mileage.
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,500 (balance shaft sprocket and timing chain replacement).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear timing chain noise or see a warning light, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The M272's balance shaft sprocket is a well-known weakness on early engines. At the first sign of timing chain noise or an engine warning light, have the system inspected immediately. Replacement of the balance shaft sprocket and timing chain is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: C220 / C230 Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Simple fix — do it before the pulley separates
Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Common on higher-mileage examples — repair when symptoms appear
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This C220 / C230 Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * W202 M111 with full service history and no timing chain rattle — durable and reliable
* W202 M112 V6 with crankshaft pulley replaced — smooth and refined
* W204 M271 turbo with full service history — the most modern option
* No rattling on startup — indicates healthy timing chain
avoidIf: * Rattling on startup on any engine — timing chain issues are common
* M112 V6 with visible rubber separation on the crankshaft pulley
* M272 V6 with engine warning light — balance shaft sprocket issues
* Poor service history — these engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The C220 and C230 petrol badges span a diverse range of Mercedes engines. The M111 (W202) is generally robust but suffers from timing chain stretch at higher mileages. The M112 V6 (W202) is smooth but prone to crankshaft pulley failure. The M271 (W203/W204) has timing chain and cam adjuster issues. The M272 V6 (W203) is known for balance shaft sprocket wear. With the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold, a £3,500–£4,500 reconditioned engine on a C-Class valued at £5,000–£10,000 is 35–90% of the car's value — assess the overall condition carefully.

cta.label: → Compare C220 / C230 petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz C220 / C230 Petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling on startup (timing chain warning on all engines)
* Service History — Including which engine era and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Crankshaft Pulley — On M112 V6, check for visible rubber separation
* Engine Warning Light — On M272, this could indicate balance shaft sprocket issues
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve cover and camshaft adjuster (M111 common)
* Performance — Test acceleration for smooth power delivery
* Supercharger Noise — On M271 Kompressor, listen for whining or unusual noises
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
